folio.label: 40v
folio.l: But sithe æat he , was fallen in the snare

note.notetxt: 45 In certain folios the first two lines are in a lighter ink. I never payed attention as I thought this difference was caused by damp, as there are stains left by dampness in all the folios so far encountered. If this is not the case, it is possible that the scribe was writing the first lines in the folio so that he would know where the lines go, and it would also explain the cramming of lines at the end of seceral versos of this tale.ODR

folio.l: He must enduren , as other folk , his care
¢ Fair was this ©ong wyf , and there w alle

note.notetxt: 47 The word ¢ Nota is written inthe left margin.Est

folio.l: As ony wesyl , hir body gent and smalle
A Seynt she wered barred , al of sylke
A barmclothe as whyte , as morwe mylke
Vp on hire lendes , ful of many a goore
White was hire smokke , and browded al byfore
And eke behynd , on hire coler a boute
Of cole blak sylk , w Inne & eke w oute
The tapes , of hire white volupere
Were of æe same seute , of hire colere
Hire filet brod of sylk , and set ful hye
And sekirly she hadde , a lykerous eye
Ful smale I pulled , were hire browes two
And tho were bent , & blake as is a slo
She was ful more blisful , on to se
Than is the newe , Pere Ionet tre
And softer æa~ne the wolle is , of a weder
And by hire girdel heng , a purs of lether

note.notetxt: heng64The ink from this word onwords changes. It becomes lighter. ODR

folio.l: Tasseled with silk , and perled w latou~
In al this world , to seken vp and dounö
There nys noman , so wys , æ coude thenche
So gay a popelot , or swiche a wenche
Ful brigútere was the shynyng , of hire hewe
Than in the Tour , the noble I forged newe
But of hire song , it was as loude , and ©erne
As ony swalwe , sittyng on a berne
Ther to she coude skyp~ , and make game
As ony kydde or calf , folwyng his dame
Hire mouth was swete , as braket or the metú
Or hoord of Apples , leyd in hey or hetú
Wynsyng she was , as is a ioly colt
Long as a mast , and vp rigút as a bolt
A broche she bar , vp on hire lowe coler
As brod , as is the boos , of a bokeler
Hire shoes were laced , on hire legges heye
She was a prymerole , a pyggesnye
For any lord , to leggen in his bedde
Or ©et , for ony good ©e~man , to wedde
¢ Now sire and eft Sire , so byfel the cas
That on a day , æis hende Nicholas
Fel with æis ©ong wyf , to rage and pleye
While that hire husbond , was at Osneye
As clerkis ben ful sotil , and ful queynt´

note.notetxt: 89After Nicholas the last three lines were crammedin a space usually occupied by two. In the right margin next to Nicholas there is a smalll dot in ink. The following folio is a parchment.ODR
